Education · Case Study

NSW Government Schools Panel: Cleaning Across Sydney Metro and Multi-Sector Education Capability

Panel cleaning supplier under the NSW Department of Education's Schools Infrastructure NSW (SINSW) framework — covering Sydney metropolitan schools, plus reactive maintenance contracts and capability across the independent education sector.

Education · NSW + Multi-Sector · Active Panel Position

  1. Home
  2. /
  3. Case Studies
  4. /
  5. NSW Government Schools Panel: Cleaning Across Sydney Metro and Multi-Sector Education Capability
200+
Sydney metro schools covered
SINSW
Active panel position
Reactive
Maintenance contracts held
Multi-sector
Government, independent, Catholic

On This Page

Progress

0%

Share

On this page

The Brief

Education facility cleaning operates under a compliance framework that most commercial cleaning environments do not encounter. Every workforce member who accesses a school must hold a current Working with Children Check (WWCC) in NSW, or a current Blue Card in Queensland, and currency must be maintained continuously through the life of the contract. A single lapsed credential for a single workforce member creates a compliance failure that constitutes a breach of the contract conditions under which access to the facility is granted.

Beyond credential compliance, school cleaning must operate around active learning environments. Term-aligned scheduling, infection control protocols during illness outbreaks, access management to keep cleaning activity separate from student movement, and the ability to respond to ad-hoc cleaning requirements — a vandalism incident, a student illness event, a facility incident requiring urgent cleaning — are operational requirements that run alongside the scheduled program.

For government school frameworks, the additional layer is the procurement framework itself. NSW Government school cleaning is not a direct contract relationship between a school and a cleaning provider — it operates through the Schools Infrastructure NSW (SINSW) panel framework, managed by Evolve FM. Panel suppliers are vetted through a formal government procurement process before they can service government schools. Active panel position is the credentialing that matters for government school procurement.

The Approach

CPC NSW's approach to education sector cleaning is built around two non-negotiable requirements: credential currency and scheduling discipline.

Credential currency — maintaining 100% WWCC compliance across all workforce members who access school sites — is managed through a continuous credential monitoring system. WWCC expiry dates are tracked, renewal processes are initiated ahead of expiry, and no workforce member accesses a school site without a verified current credential. This is not a reactive process triggered by an annual audit — it is a continuous management function.

Scheduling discipline in school environments means term-aware planning: recognising that the cleaning requirements of term time, school holiday periods, and exam periods are materially different, and that the cleaning program must respond to the school's calendar rather than a fixed commercial schedule. Holiday period deep cleans, pre-term preparation, and event-based cleaning requirements are planned and resourced as part of the scheduling framework, not treated as exceptions.

Reactive maintenance capability under the SINSW framework requires maintaining the operational flexibility to respond to ad-hoc service requests within the government SLA timeframes — typically same-day or next-business-day response for routine reactive requests, and immediate response for health and safety incidents.

The Operation

CPC NSW operates as a panel cleaning supplier under the NSW Department of Education's Schools Infrastructure NSW (SINSW) framework, managed by Evolve FM. The panel coverage spans the Sydney metropolitan area, including schools across groups including Beverly Hills, Bondi, Botany Bay, Canterbury, Georges River, Inner Sydney, Iron Cove, Kogarah, Marrickville, Port Hacking, Port Jackson, Strathfield, Sutherland, and Woronora River among others. The panel covers primary schools, secondary schools, infants schools, schools for specific purposes and environmental education centres — reflecting the range of facility types that fall within the NSW Department of Education estate.

The 200+ Sydney metropolitan schools covered under the panel framework represent a cross-section of facility types, student populations and operational schedules that require a cleaning program capable of adapting to each school's specific context, not a standardised commercial approach applied uniformly.

Beyond the government school panel, CPC's education sector capability spans the independent school sector and the Catholic education sector. Multi-sector capability means simultaneously managing different governance frameworks — the SINSW framework's procurement and reporting requirements, independent school bodies' individual contract requirements, and Catholic diocesan education authorities' procurement standards — each with their own credential verification, scheduling and performance reporting expectations.

Compliance and Standards

The compliance framework for CPC's education portfolio operates across the following dimensions:

  • SINSW Panel conditions. NSW Department of Education procurement framework requirements, including all panel supplier obligations for performance, credential currency, reporting and reactive maintenance response under the Evolve FM-managed framework.
  • Working with Children Check (NSW). Mandatory WWCC credential for all workforce members accessing NSW school sites, with continuous currency monitoring and zero-tolerance for credential gaps at point of site access.
  • Blue Card (Queensland). Equivalent credential requirement for Queensland education sector engagements, managed under the same continuous monitoring system as NSW WWCC credentials.
  • ISO 9001:2015. Quality Management System certification, applied to education sector service delivery including scheduled cleaning programs, reactive maintenance response, and performance documentation.
  • Infection control protocols. School-specific cleaning product selection, surface disinfection procedures and enhanced cleaning protocols for gastroenteritis, respiratory illness and other communicable disease incidents managed within active school environments.

Outcomes

Active SINSW panel position is the primary outcome evidence for government education sector compliance. Panel frameworks do not retain non-performing or non-compliant suppliers — the formal government procurement process that establishes panel position is designed to identify providers who can meet the compliance and performance requirements continuously.

The reactive maintenance contract portfolio within the SINSW framework provides evidence of operational flexibility alongside the scheduled program — the ability to respond within government SLA timeframes, dispatch a workforce to an ad-hoc requirement, and document completion in a format that meets the framework's reporting requirements.

Multi-sector capability across government, independent and Catholic education demonstrates the governance adaptability that large education sector clients require. A provider active across all three sectors simultaneously has demonstrated the ability to maintain different compliance frameworks concurrently — the most demanding test of education sector operational maturity.

What This Demonstrates

Key Takeaways

  • Panel position on NSW Government's school cleaning framework demonstrates compliance with public sector education procurement standards — a formal government vetting process, not self-assessed capability.
  • Working with Children Check compliance must be 100% at all times — a single credential gap for any workforce member accessing a school is a contract termination event, not a minor administrative issue.
  • Reactive maintenance contracts under the SINSW framework prove operational flexibility for ad-hoc service delivery alongside scheduled cleaning — the ability to respond within government SLAs when an event, incident or facility issue requires urgent cleaning.
  • Multi-sector capability across government, independent and Catholic education requires navigating different governance frameworks simultaneously, with different credential requirements, scheduling constraints and reporting obligations per sector.
  • For procurement teams in education, active SINSW panel position signals readiness for both large-scale contracts and rapid mobilisation — it is the most direct proxy for education sector compliance readiness available.

Why This Matters for Procurement

For education procurement teams, SINSW panel position is the fastest proxy for compliance readiness — it signals that CPC NSW has been vetted through a formal government procurement process, maintains WWCC currency for all relevant workforce members, and can deliver reactive maintenance obligations within government SLAs. For independent and Catholic school procurement, simultaneous active capability across multiple governance frameworks provides a reference that single-sector providers cannot match. The relevant question is not which provider claims education sector experience — it is which provider holds an active government panel position and can demonstrate reactive maintenance delivery history.
